title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 15 (Fragment 3)
text:
The wind passed, wailing like a wolf. They raised their heads into the sun.

The Duchess was standing on the platform in front of them. She had melting ice winking and trickling from her hair and from every fold of her marble-gray dress. The smile on her waxy face was not pleasant.

“Oh no,” she said. “The Angel is not going to help anyone this time. Did you think I’d for gotten?”

Marco and Rosa looked up at the Angel’s golden arm holding the great scroll above them. If they had not understood before, they knew now. From their suddenly thoughtful faces, Tonino knew they were finding spells to use on the Duchess.

“Don’t!” squeaked Angelica. “She’s an enchantress!”

The Duchess’s lips pursed in another unpleasant little smile. “More than that,” she said. She pointed up at the Angel. “Let the words be removed from the scroll,” she said.

There was a click from the huge golden statue, followed by a grating sound, as if a spring had been released. The arm holding the scroll began to move, gently and steadily downwards, making the slightest grinding noise as it moved. They could hear it easily, in spite of a sudden clatter of gunfire from the houses beyond the river. Downwards and inwards, traveled the Angel’s arm, until it stopped with a small clunk . The scroll now hung, flashing in the sun, between them and the Duchess. There were large raised letters on it. Angelus , they saw. Capronensi populo . It was as if the Angel were holding it out for them to read.

“Exactly,” said the Duchess, though Tonino thought, from the surprised arch of her eyebrows, that this was not at all what she expected. She pointed again at the scroll, with a long white finger like a white wax pencil. “Erase,” she said. “Word by word.”

Their heads all tipped anxiously as they looked at the lines of writing. The first word read Carmen . And, sure enough, the golden capital C was sinking slowly away into the metal background. Paolo moved. He had to do something. The Duchess glanced at him, a contemptuous flick of the eyebrows. Paolo found he was twisted to the spot, with jabbing cramp in both legs.

But he could still speak, and he remembered what Marco and Rosa had said last night. Without daring to draw breath, he screamed as loud as he could. “Chrestomanci!”

There was more wind. This was one keen blaring gust. And Chrestomanci was there, beyond Renata and the cats. There was so little room on the platform that Chrestomanci rocked, and quickly took hold of the marble balustrade. He was still in uniform, but it was muddy and he looked extremely tired.

The Duchess whirled around and pointed her long finger at him. “You! I misled you!”

“Oh you did,” Chrestomanci said. If the Duchess had hoped to catch him off balance, she was too late. Chrestomanci was steady now. “You led me a proper wild-goose chase,” he said, and put out one hand, palm forward, towards her pointing finger. The long finger bent and began dripping white, as if it were wax indeed. The Duchess stared at it, and then looked up at Chrestomanci almost imploringly. “No,” Chrestomanci said, sounding very tired. “I think you’ve done enough harm. Take your true form, please.” He beckoned at her, like someone sick of waiting.

Instantly, the Duchess’s body was seething out of shape. Her arms gathered inwards. Her face lengthened, and yet still remained the same waxy, sardonic face. Whiskers sprang from her upper lip, and her eyes lit red, like bulging lamps. Her marble skirts turned white, billowed and gathered soapily to her ankles, revealing her feet as long pink claws. And all the time, she was shrinking. Two teeth appeared at the end of her lengthened white face. A naked pink tail, marked in rings like an earthworm, snaked from behind the soapy bundle of her skirts and lashed the marble floor angrily. She shrank again.

vocabulary.definition: made a gesture to encourage someone to come nearer or follow
vocabulary.example: ” He beckoned at her, like someone sick of waiting.

vocabulary.definition: moved or flowed outward with a rolling motion
vocabulary.example: Her marble skirts turned white, billowed and gathered soapily to her ankles, revealing her feet as long pink claws.

vocabulary.definition: making a loud and harsh sound
vocabulary.example: This was one keen blaring gust.

vocabulary.definition: a continuous rattling sound as of hard objects striking together
vocabulary.example: They could hear it easily, in spite of a sudden clatter of gunfire from the houses beyond the river.

vocabulary.definition: the sharp curved nails on the feet of some animals
vocabulary.example: Her marble skirts turned white, billowed and gathered soapily to her ankles, revealing her feet as long pink claws.

vocabulary.definition: showing deep hatred or disapproval
vocabulary.example: The Duchess glanced at him, a contemptuous flick of the eyebrows.

vocabulary.definition: a painful, involuntary contraction of a muscle
vocabulary.example: Paolo found he was twisted to the spot, with jabbing cramp in both legs.

vocabulary.definition: a woman who uses magic or witchcraft to put spells on people
vocabulary.example: “She’s an enchantress!

vocabulary.definition: harsh and unpleasant to hear
vocabulary.example: There was a click from the huge golden statue, followed by a grating sound, as if a spring had been released.

vocabulary.definition: in a way that makes a sincere or emotional appeal
vocabulary.example: The Duchess stared at it, and then looked up at Chrestomanci almost imploringly.

vocabulary.definition: made longer
vocabulary.example: Her face lengthened, and yet still remained the same waxy, sardonic face.

vocabulary.definition: becoming liquid as a result of heat
vocabulary.example: She had melting ice winking and trickling from her hair and from every fold of her marble-gray dress.

vocabulary.definition: without any covering or clothing
vocabulary.example: A naked pink tail, marked in rings like an earthworm, snaked from behind the soapy bundle of her skirts and lashed the marble floor angrily.

vocabulary.definition: giving a sense of happy satisfaction or enjoyment
vocabulary.example: The smile on her waxy face was not pleasant.

vocabulary.definition: pressed the lips together tightly, often showing disapproval or displeasure
vocabulary.example:
”

The Duchess’s lips pursed in another unpleasant little smile.

vocabulary.definition: moved gently back and forth or from side to side
vocabulary.example: There was so little room on the platform that Chrestomanci rocked, and quickly took hold of the marble balustrade.

vocabulary.definition: grimly mocking or cynical
vocabulary.example: Her face lengthened, and yet still remained the same waxy, sardonic face.

vocabulary.definition: to make a loud, sharp, piercing cry
vocabulary.example: Without daring to draw breath, he screamed as loud as he could.

vocabulary.definition: a rolled-up piece of parchment or paper with writing on it
vocabulary.example:
”

Marco and Rosa looked up at the Angel’s golden arm holding the great scroll above them.

vocabulary.definition: filled with intense but unexpressed anger
vocabulary.example: Instantly, the Duchess’s body was seething out of shape.

vocabulary.definition: became smaller in size
vocabulary.example: She shrank again.

vocabulary.definition: moved in a winding or twisting manner
vocabulary.example: A naked pink tail, marked in rings like an earthworm, snaked from behind the soapy bundle of her skirts and lashed the marble floor angrily.

vocabulary.definition: in a regular and even manner
vocabulary.example: The arm holding the scroll began to move, gently and steadily downwards, making the slightest grinding noise as it moved.

vocabulary.definition: firmly fixed, supported, or balanced; not shaking
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci was steady now.

vocabulary.definition: flowing in a small stream or drops
vocabulary.example: She had melting ice winking and trickling from her hair and from every fold of her marble-gray dress.

vocabulary.definition: to make a long, high, sad cry, usually because of pain or sadness
vocabulary.example: The wind passed, wailing like a wolf.
